characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: All Girls Like Ponies: She loves horses and it's later revealed that she liked horseback riding before her mother's death. Book Dumb: Mary-Kate needs a tutor because she hates studying and never pays attention in class. However, she has more common sense than herBook Smartbut shallow sister Ashley. Bratty Teenage Daughter: In a different way from Ashley. Mary-Kate is snarky, brash, rebellious, and doesn't take school seriously. Expy: She is quite similar to Al Lambert (mainly the tomboyish Al of the early seasons) fromStep by Step, the previous series produced by Miller-Boyett. Even some of their storylines are the same, like being selected as a model instead of the girly sister. Generation Xerox: It's mentioned thather late motherwas athletic like her. And they both liked riding horses.

characters.name: Mary-Kate Burke
characters.description: All Girls Like Ponies: She loves horses and it's later revealed that she liked horseback riding before her mother's death. Book Dumb: Mary-Kate needs a tutor because she hates studying and never pays attention in class. However, she has more common sense than herBook Smartbut shallow sister Ashley. Bratty Teenage Daughter: In a different way from Ashley. Mary-Kate is snarky, brash, rebellious, and doesn't take school seriously. Expy: She is quite similar to Al Lambert (mainly the tomboyish Al of the early seasons) fromStep by Step, the previous series produced by Miller-Boyett. Even some of their storylines are the same, like being selected as a model instead of the girly sister. Generation Xerox: It's mentioned thather late motherwas athletic like her. And they both liked riding horses.

characters.name: Ashley Burke
characters.description: TheGirly Girlof the Burke twin sisters. Athletically Challenged: Unlike her sister, Ashley is unathletic, is alwaysPicked Lastin gym class, and has zero interest in sports. Book Smart: Unlike her sister, Ashley is a straight A student, and the lowest grade she has ever gotten is a B+. Bratty Teenage Daughter: She tends to obsess over clothes and cute boys, and can be quite selfish. Ditzy Genius: Ashley is academically smart, but a bit superficial and ditzy. Mary-Kate often shows more common sense than her.

characters.name: Kevin Burke
characters.description: A college professor and father of the twins. Butt-Monkey: He can often find himself in embarrassing situations, usually thanks to Carrie. Deadpan Snarker: All the time. He constantly snarks at everyone, especially Carrie. His daughterMary-Katetook this trait from him. Foolish Sibling, Responsible Sibling: Kevin is a responsible and hard-working college professor. His brother Matt is an immature slacker, much to Kevin's frustration. Not So Above It All: He tries to be the voice of reason, but also has moments where he acts in an immature way.

characters.name: Carrie Moore
characters.description: Kevin's student and the babysitter of the twins. Back to School: The story begins with her enrolling in Kevin's class after a long period of travel and self-discovery, which explains why she's a bit older than the typical college student. Bunny-Ears Lawyer: Carrie is eccentric but a great babysitter. Cool Big Sis: She is in her 20s and acts like as as big sister to the twins, often supporting them when they need it. Deadpan Snarker: Not as much as Kevin and Mary-Kate, but Carrie also snarks quite often around Kevin.

characters.name: Max and Brian
characters.description: Two friends of the twins, mostly Mary-Kate. Chuck Cunningham Syndrome: Both are recurring characters before disappearing with no explanation in the second half of the series. Hopeless Suitor: Brian is kind of a loser, so his crush on Ashley is completely unrequited. Those Two Guys: They always appear together and never have a major roles. Token Black Friend: Max is one of Mary-Kate's friends and the only black recurring character. He doesn't have much screentime aside from his duo with Brian.

characters.name: Eddie Fairbanks
characters.description: Best Friend: To Kevin, they have been best friends for years, despite their different personalities. Chuck Cunningham Syndrome: He starts out as a regular member before disappearing about midway through the series' run without further mention. Drop-In Character: In the first half of the series, he regularly shows up at the Burke's house, being a friend of Kevin. He often bickers with Carrie. Sensitive Guy and Manly Man: The Manly Man (a plumber andCasanova Wannabe) to Kevin's Sensitive Guy (an intellectual professor).

characters.name: Matt Burke
characters.description: Kevin's brother who moves in with his family in later episodes. Foolish Sibling, Responsible Sibling: The Foolish. Kevin is a responsible and hard-working college professor, while Matt is an immature slacker, much to Kevin's frustration. Jerk with a Heart of Gold: He is irresponsible and selfish, but aCool Uncleto Ashley and Mary-Kate. Sensitive Guy and Manly Man: The Manly Man (more macho and athletic) to Kevin's Sensitive Guy (an intellectual professor) Suspiciously Similar Substitute: Matt is introduced after Eddie's disappearance and has a similar role. Another adult male who is more cocky, immature, andBook Dumbcompared to Kevin.

characters.name: Paul
characters.description: Another of Kevin's students and a classmate of Carrie. Butt-Monkey: He is quite pathetic, and there are many jokes at his expense. Cannot Spit It Out: Paul has a crush on Carrie, but is too afraid to confess it to her. Geek Physiques: He is a geeky student who is socially awkward around people, and this is also emphasized by his tall and lanky appearance with barely much (or any) sign of muscle on his body.
